evidence_quotes.quote: censorship of games and other online media by payment processors

paragraphs.text: Hi there, First and foremost, I'd like to say thank you for being given the opportunity to share any form of feedback when it comes to consumer rights, especially digital rights - given the significant part it plays almost everyone's day-to-day lives. A major issue I believe should be tackled as part of this act is the ongoing dispute surrounding censorship of games and other online media by payment processors such as MasterCard and VISA, spurred by a petition from the seemingly well meaning "Collective Shout" organisation. "Well meaning", however, seems to be far from reality - the group has had numerous instances of attempting to suppress and establish limits on various forms of art - be it music or games, as well as other forms of media, such as movies, ads and magazines. I do not wish to make any generalised claims on the matter, but as a personal opinion - Collective Shout is a ham-fisted attempt at censoring several pieces of media as part of very thinly veiled religious "crusade" Melinda Tankard Reist - the co-founder of Collective Shout has had several connections to Christian Baptist Churches and is a prominent enough figure in those circles. I don't find it reasonable to censor media due to someone's religious and general ideologies about life. Ms Reist is also what can be considered a "Pro-Life" advocate and as such is, in reality, "Anti-Choice". The requests, and frankly the demands from Collective Shout and Ms Reist are a slippery slope that can and most likely will lead to the suppression and censorship of LGBTQIA+ representation in most major forms of media. The veiled demands that this movement is against a number of vile representations in games is just the first step of many. As an example - Ms Reist and her movement also attempted to take down a video game "Detroit: Become Human" due to depictions of domestic abuse. This censorship is not meant to protect anyone, as someone who has seen said depictions, this was nothing more than social commentary and did not glorify or romanticise a very, very dark topic. It's also become apparent that Collective Shout are not above supporting or at least agreeing with Trans Exclusive Radical Feminits (TERFs) which once against - raises extreme concerns about how far they are willing to go in order to push their own views upon everyone globally. This is a serious concern that shouldn't be taken lightly. Groups like Collective Shout should not be allow to dictate what is deemed appropriate for users, based on their own religious and personal views. I am once again going to stress that this is a slippery slope that will result in radical and fundamental discrimination towards members of the LGBTQIA+ community. Groups like Collective Shout should not be given this amount of power, considering the small scope of their organisation. A vocal minority should not have control over fundamental issues like these. In conclusion, I believe that censorship of digital media (in this case, games) by payment processors on behalf of religiously charged movements will lead to significant discrimination against minorities in the long run and will affect media and the online space negatively in more ways than one. I understand that this is undoubtedly an "edge case" considering the general goal of the Digital Fairness Act, however, I wholeheartedly believe that it should be addressed as it falls under the category of "harmful practices by influencers" - even if the influencers in this instance are a whole organisation, as opposed to single individuals. I'd like to once again thank you for the opportunity presented here. I understand that this is a significant block of text, but I do find these matters to be rather important.
